portfolio_view: These five span energy refining, two pharma sub-themes, specialty chemicals, and auto components — real sector spread, with three names independently verified by strong FY26 print acceleration (CHENNPETRO, NAVINFLUOR, LUPIN) and two on differentiated pharma stories (EMCURE global partnerships, LUPIN complex-generics value). CHENNPETRO doubles as a confirmation signal — the daily-analyst layer also flagged it BUY/HIGH/FULL PLAN on 09 Jul. Note on daily-analyst BUYs not selected here: SYRMA and SHILPAMED are excluded solely because the risk engine found no tradeable plan (too volatile) — not a story rejection; LAURUSLABS (BUY/MEDIUM) is passed over because its conviction/technicals rank below this list and healthcare is already covered twice by EMCURE and LUPIN's more distinct theses.

meta.highlights: smart-money 100: FII 8.8->14.98%; DII 2.6->1.01%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C); technicals/RS 96: RS percentile 92; TT 8/8, stage 2, VCP live; balance-sheet 91: deleveraging: debt 4260 -> 1964 Cr; earnings 91: qtr PAT YoY +200%; accelerating (TTM 1349% > 3y -4%)

picks.selected_because: Highest conviction (72.5) in the pool, smart-money score maxed (FII 8.8%→14.98%), earnings inflecting hard (qtr PAT +200% YoY), and independently confirmed BUY/HIGH/FULL PLAN by the daily-analyst layer on 09 Jul.
picks.thesis: Refining margins are the real driver: Indian OMC/refiner GRMs rebounded to $8-13/bbl in Q2-Q3 FY26 as the Urals-Brent discount widened to ~$7/bbl, directly cutting CPCL's feedstock cost. FY26 EPS jumped to ~₹208 with a 540% final dividend, and debt nearly halved (₹4,260cr→₹1,964cr).
picks.catalyst: Q1 FY27 board meeting scheduled 23 Jul 2026 — imminent print.
picks.management: PSU (IOC-promoted) refiner; no governance flags; deleveraging is real and verifiable.
picks.risks: Pure margin cyclical — GRM depends on the Urals discount holding as India reconfigures crude sourcing under US sanctions pressure; a base-effect earnings comp (TTM 1349% vs 3yr -4%) means growth optics will normalize sharply.
picks.watch_for: Urals discount narrowing back below ~$3-4/bbl would compress the core margin thesis fast.

meta.highlights: smart-money 100: FII 2.86->3.35%; DII 2.84->6.1%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C); balance-sheet 87: deleveraging: debt 2334 -> 1558 Cr; governance 85: no pledge disclosed; auditor/SEBI/related-party checks pending (Phase C); earnings 82: qtr PAT YoY +24%; accelerating (TTM 38% > 3y 21%)

picks.selected_because: Second-highest conviction (69.4) with clean balance-sheet deleveraging (₹2,334cr→₹1,558cr) and accelerating earnings (qtr PAT +24%, TTM 38% > 3yr 21%); international mix (60% of revenue) reduces single-market risk.
picks.thesis: Crossed $1B revenue in FY26 on 22% international growth; new Novo Nordisk semaglutide distribution and Roche nephrology in-licensing (Jan 2026) open GLP-1 and complex-therapy revenue lines beyond the base generics business. Jefferies models 20% EPS CAGR FY26-29E on the injectables/biosimilar pipeline.
picks.catalyst: Semaglutide distribution ramp and Roche nephrology brand launches through 2H26.
picks.management: No pledge disclosed; recently public (post-IPO), so track record as a listed entity is still short — auditor/related-party checks are explicitly pending in this data.
picks.risks: Newly listed company with limited public-market history; partnership-dependent growth (Novo/Roche) carries execution risk if ramp is slower than modeled.
picks.watch_for: International segment growth decelerating below ~15% would undercut the core re-rating case.

meta.highlights: earnings 100: qtr PAT YoY +124%; accelerating (TTM 132% > 3y 22%); technicals/RS 95: RS percentile 88; TT 8/8, stage 2, VCP live; governance 85: no pledge disclosed; auditor/SEBI/related-party checks pending (Phase C); smart-money 69: FII 21.55->23.78%; DII 28.7->27.61%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C)

picks.selected_because: Top earnings score in the pool (100: qtr PAT +124%, accelerating), RS percentile 88, VCP live — strong and balanced across dimensions with six consecutive quarters of growth confirmed independently.
picks.thesis: China's 2026 refrigerant quota rose only marginally (~0.7%), effectively freezing domestic HFC capacity there and pushing Chinese refrigerant prices to record highs — a direct tailwind for Navin Fluorine's specialty fluorochemical and refrigerant-gas exports to Africa/Middle East/South Asia, markets where HFC phase-down lags China by years. FY26 net profit +130% YoY to ₹663cr on 41% revenue growth.
picks.catalyst: Q1 FY27 results due 5 Aug 2026; CDMO/MPP capacity ramp.
picks.management: No pledge; established specialty-chem franchise with long CDMO relationships.
picks.risks: Valuation has already re-rated hard on this run; agrochemical-linked demand is cyclical and DII holding trimmed slightly (28.7%→27.61%).
picks.watch_for: A reversal in China's quota discipline (large capacity add) would erode the pricing tailwind.

meta.highlights: smart-money 100: FII 6.78->8.86%; DII 7.12->10.16%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C); technicals/RS 82: RS percentile 94; TT 8/8, stage 2, VCP none; balance-sheet 82: deleveraging: debt 2353 -> 1521 Cr; equity capital +2125% over 3y — check bonus/split vs g; earnings 81: qtr PAT YoY +18%; accelerating (TTM 41% > 3y 17%)

picks.selected_because: Full smart-money score (FII 6.78%→8.86%, DII 7.12%→10.16%), RS percentile 94, and real deleveraging (₹2,353cr→₹1,521cr) — Jefferies confirms with a target upgrade to ₹250.
picks.thesis: Belrise supplies safety-critical chassis, BIW, suspension and exhaust systems into 2-wheeler OEMs riding an export boom — India 2W exports grew 23.4% in FY26, with Bajaj (+17.5%) and TVS (+30.9%, record monthly volumes in May) both accelerating, a direct volume pass-through to component suppliers.
picks.catalyst: A recent QIP (floor ₹230.79) funds capacity; watch for utilization ramp disclosure.
picks.management: No pledge disclosed; equity capital +2,125% over 3yrs reflects recent IPO-stage capital structure — verify this isn't masking dilution before sizing.
picks.risks: Fresh QIP creates near-term supply/overhang; earnings growth (qtr +18%) is the softest of the five picks relative to its RS.
picks.watch_for: 2W OEM volume growth rolling over would remove the core demand pull-through.

meta.highlights: valuation 95: P/E 20 cheaper than growth (PEG 0.14); governance 85: no pledge disclosed; auditor/SEBI/related-party checks pending (Phase C); earnings 76: qtr PAT YoY +88%; decelerating (TTM 78% < 3y 141%); technicals/RS 75: RS percentile 77; TT 8/8, stage 2, VCP none

picks.selected_because: Best valuation score in the pool (PEG 0.14, PE 20) — a quality compounder trading cheap relative to growth, with governance clean and TT 8/8 stage-2 confirmed.
picks.thesis: FY26 revenue +24%, profit +62%, EBITDA margin 33.6%, net cash >₹4,600cr — genuine quality, not just cheapness. Fresh FDA tentative approvals (Pitolisant, generic Enzalutamide for prostate cancer) show the complex-generics pipeline converting to launches, the right response to persistent US commodity-generic price erosion.
picks.catalyst: Enzalutamide and further complex-ANDA launches through 2H26.
picks.management: No pledge; disciplined balance sheet with net cash position.
picks.risks: Machine flags earnings deceleration (TTM 78% < 3yr 141%); US exports 42% of sales — generics are tariff-exempt now but a one-year review clause leaves that exemption unconfirmed longer-term.
picks.watch_for:
Any signal the US tariff review extends 100% duties to generics would hit this thesis directly.
